title: Heat Dome Business Continuity Playbook — Texas Operations
trigger: Forecast: Tₕₐₓ ≥ 100 °F for 3+ consecutive days AND ERCOT Conservation Appeal/EEA AND cooling-tower approach > 8 °F above design.
tier1_actions: Cleanroom fabs (Austin Fab 1/2): pre-cool process water reservoirs overnight; shift non-critical maintenance to overnight; defer wafer starts needing extra cooling-tower capacity.
Assembly & sub-assembly (San Antonio, Waco): reduce ambient setpoints by 2 °F in reflow-oven test floors; stage +1-shift sub-assembly buffer to absorb upstream delay.
Distribution / logistics (Dallas, Houston, Corpus, Lubbock): move outdoor loading to 04:00–10:00; verify reefer container fuel reserves at Houston port docks.
tier2_actions: Curtail Austin Fab 2 non-critical production lines by 15% at ERCOT EEA-2.
Shift R&D campus to remote work; close auxiliary HVAC zones.
Activate mutual aid agreement with Phoenix backup distribution.
stand_down: 24 hours after ERCOT alert clears AND forecast highs drop below 95 °F for 48 hours. Lessons-learned within 5 business days; playbook update within 30 days.
summary: Tier-1 actions trigger when forecast ≥100 °F for 3+ days + ERCOT alert + cooling-tower approach >8 °F above design. Pre-cool reservoirs, defer wafer starts, raise setpoints, move outdoor loading to early-morning. Tier-2 (EEA-2+) curtails Austin Fab 2 by 15% and activates Phoenix mutual aid.

title: Austin Fab Cooling Tower Operations — Heat Event SOP
trigger: Wet-bulb temperature forecast ≥ 80 °F OR cooling-tower approach trending >6 °F above design.
tier1_actions: Switch evaporator fans to high before peak afternoon load.
Bias process chiller setpoint up 1 °F to widen approach margin (validated for nodes ≥28 nm).
Verify make-up water tank levels ≥ 80%; refill from rainwater cistern overnight if available.
tier2_actions: Stage portable chilled-water trailers at Fab 2 loading dock if approach exceeds 10 °F.
Coordinate with City of Austin Water on emergency make-up rate; daily check-in with utility liaison.
stand_down: Approach returns within 4 °F of design for 24 hours; trailers demobilized after 48 hours of normal operation.
summary: Cooling-tower SOP: fans-high pre-peak, +1 °F chiller setpoint, verify make-up water ≥80%. Stage portable trailers if approach > 10 °F. Coordinate emergency make-up with Austin Water.

title: Wildfire / Smoke Response — West Texas Logistics
trigger: Forecast PM2.5 ≥ 35 µg/m³ OR US AQI ≥ 150 OR active fire perimeter within 25 mi.
tier1_actions: Distribute N95 respirators to outdoor workers; relocate breakroom outdoors-access tasks indoors.
Switch building HVAC to maximum filtration (MERV-13+); seal exterior dock doors when idle.
Reroute long-haul outbound from I-20/I-10 corridor through alternate northern route via Lubbock.
tier2_actions: Suspend outdoor packaging operations at El Paso if PM2.5 ≥ 55 µg/m³.
Activate shelter-in-place for Lubbock logistics if active fire within 10 mi.
stand_down: PM2.5 < 12 µg/m³ sustained for 12 hours and no active fire within 25 mi.
summary: Wildfire SOP for West TX: N95s + MERV-13 HVAC + sealed docks. Reroute long-haul off I-20/I-10 when smoky. Suspend El Paso outdoor packaging at PM2.5 ≥ 55. Shelter-in-place threshold: fire within 10 mi of Lubbock.

title: Wafer Flow Supply-Chain Continuity
trigger: Austin Fab 1 or Fab 2 forecast to drop wafer starts ≥15% for 3+ days OR El Paso packaging projected offline ≥48 hours.
tier1_actions: Front-load San Antonio test floor with +12 hours of WIP inventory.
Hold Waco sub-assembly to standard tact until upstream recovery confirmed (avoid stranded WIP).
Notify Tier-1 customers of potential 5-7 day lead-time slip on AI accelerator SKUs.
tier2_actions: Activate alternate packaging at Phoenix facility for highest-margin SKUs.
Air-freight critical lots San Antonio → El Paso when ground transit at risk.
stand_down: Austin fabs running at ≥95% nominal wafer starts AND El Paso packaging back at full capacity.
summary: Wafer-flow continuity: pre-stage +12h WIP at San Antonio test, hold Waco sub-assembly at standard tact, notify Tier-1 customers of 5-7d slip. Tier-2: Phoenix alt-packaging + air freight for critical lots.

title: Houston Port Distribution Center Continuity
trigger: Houston DC offline ≥ 24 hours OR Port of Houston closure OR reefer power loss ≥3 hours.
tier1_actions: Reroute Asia-Pacific inbound containers to Port of Corpus Christi DC.
Stand up Dallas DC as overflow hub for domestic outbound; pre-stage drivers.
Notify reefer monitoring service to dispatch backup gensets within 90 minutes of power loss.
tier2_actions: Coordinate with Port Authority on priority berth allocation when reopening.
Activate freight forwarder air-cargo contracts for time-sensitive lots.
stand_down: Houston DC restored to ≥90% throughput AND inbound queue ≤ 24 hours of normal volume.
summary: Houston port disruption: reroute APAC inbound to Corpus Christi DC, stand up Dallas as domestic overflow, deploy backup reefer gensets in <90 min. Tier-2: priority berth coordination, air-cargo activation for time-critical lots.
